name: code-review-graph
install_doc: README: `pip install code-review-graph` (we pin 2.3.6 via `uv tool install`, isolated). The README's `code-review-graph install` is NOT used: it writes into the *current* project (MCP config, .claude hooks, a git pre-commit hook, a CLAUDE.md block) regardless of HOME — host/repo pollution. Instead we transcribe the MCP entry it writes verbatim (`code-review-graph serve`, stdio) and run the per-repo graph build in setup_commands so indexing cost lands in setup_ms.
notes: Tree-sitter AST knowledge graph; claims 6.8-49x fewer tokens on review tasks. MCP server validated via initialize+tools/list handshake (12+ ctx tools). Build runs per workspace; on non-code workspaces the graph is near-empty (honest: nothing to index).
